claim: Polylogue can regenerate a bounded handoff read package for the large Sinex devloop peer session using exact-session temporal and chronicle projections over the current archive.
non_claim: This is not a full transcript export and not proof that action follow-up classification is cheap for huge sessions; temporal uses lightweight occurrence evidence deliberately.
proof: temporal.json and chronicle.json were generated against /home/sinity/.local/share/polylogue schema v23
focused read-view tests passed for exact-id summary resolution and lightweight temporal action occurrence reads
live EXPLAIN showed the action occurrence query anchored by idx_blocks_session_position and idx_blocks_tool_id
caveat: message_events_capped and action_events_capped mean the packet is intentionally bounded
project_actions still dominates this large-session packet at roughly 6.75s and remains a performance target
